categories.name: حُرُوفُ ٱلْعَطْفِ وَٱلِٱنتِقَالِ
categories.nameEn: Particles used for conjunction and transition

items.verseEn: Then He causes his death and provides a grave for him.
But you prefer the worldly life,

items.verse: أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ وَرَتِّلِ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ تَرْتِيلًا
items.verseEn: Or add to it, and recite the Qur’ān with measured recitation.

items.verse: أَمْ خُلِقُوا۟ مِنْ غَيْرِ شَىْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُونَ
items.verseEn: Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators [of themselves]?

categories.name: أَسْمَاءٌ تَصِفُ وَسَائِلَ أَوْ أَحْوَالَ الْعَذَابِ الْإِلَٰهِيِّ
categories.nameEn: Nouns describing means or states of divine punishment

items.verseEn: Striking them with stones of hard clay,

items.verse: وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًۭا فَقَالَ ٱلضُّعَفَـٰٓؤُا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًۭا فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّغْنُونَ عَنَّا مِنْ عَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍۢ ۚ قَالُوا۟ لَوْ هَدَىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ لَهَدَيْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْنَآ أَجَزِعْنَآ أَمْ صَبَرْنَا مَا لَنَا مِن مَّحِيصٍۢ
items.verseEn: And they will come out [for judgement] before Allāh all together, and the weak will say to those who were arrogant, "Indeed, we were your followers, so can you avail us anything against the punishment of Allāh?" They will say, "If Allāh had guided us, we would have guided you. It is all the same for us whether we show intolerance or are patient: there is for us no place of escape."

items.verse: وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ فَمَآ أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ءَالِهَتُهُمُ ٱلَّتِى يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍۢ لَّمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا زَادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍۢ
items.verseEn: And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than Allāh when there came the command of your Lord. And they did not increase them in other than ruin.

items.verse: يُرْسَلُ عَلَيْكُمَا شُوَاظٌۭ مِّن نَّارٍۢ وَنُحَاسٌۭ فَلَا تَنتَصِرَانِ
items.verseEn: There will be sent upon you a flame of fire and smoke, and you will not defend yourselves.

categories.name: أَصْنَامٌ وَآلِهَةٌ بَاطِلَةٌ
categories.nameEn: Objects of false worship mentioned by name

items.verseEn: So have you considered al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā?
And Manāt, the third - the other one?
Do you call upon Baʿl and leave the best of creators -

items.verse: وَٱتَّخَذَ قَوْمُ مُوسَىٰ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ مِنْ حُلِيِّهِمْ عِجْلًۭا جَسَدًۭا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌ ۚ أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّهُۥ لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ وَلَا يَهْدِيهِمْ سَبِيلًا ۘ ٱتَّخَذُوهُ وَكَانُوا۟ ظَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And the people of Moses made, after [his departure], from their ornaments a calf - an image having a lowing sound. Did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to a way? They took it [for worship], and they were wrongdoers.

categories.name: أَسْمَاءٌ مُؤَنَّثَةٌ تَأْنِيثًا سَمَاعِيًّا
categories.nameEn: Nouns that are grammatically feminine by convention (mu'annath samā'ī)

items.verse: فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَأْذَنُوا۟ بِحَرْبٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۖ وَإِن تُبْتُمْ فَلَكُمْ رُءُوسُ أَمْوَٰلِكُمْ لَا تَظْلِمُونَ وَلَا تُظْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: And if you do not, then be informed of a war [against you] from Allāh and His Messenger. But if you repent, you may have your principal - [thus] you do no wrong, nor are you wronged.

items.verse: ثُمَّ فِى سِلْسِلَةٍۢ ذَرْعُهَا سَبْعُونَ ذِرَاعًۭا فَٱسْلُكُوهُ
items.verseEn: Then into a chain whose length is seventy cubits insert him."

items.verse: أَكَانَ لِلنَّاسِ عَجَبًا أَنْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ رَجُلٍۢ مِّنْهُمْ أَنْ أَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَنَّ لَهُمْ قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ قَالَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌۭ مُّبِينٌ
items.verseEn: Have the people been amazed that We revealed [revelation] to a man from among them, [saying], "Warn mankind and give good tidings to those who believe that they will have a [firm] precedence of honor with their Lord"? [But] the disbelievers say, "Indeed, this is an obvious magician."

items.verse: وَلَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلَىٰ عُنُقِكَ وَلَا تَبْسُطْهَا كُلَّ ٱلْبَسْطِ فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُومًۭا مَّحْسُورًا
items.verseEn: And do not make your hand [as] chained to your neck or extend it completely and [thereby] become blamed and insolvent.

harf.hint: Closer to you than this life-giving vessel.
harf.arabicVerse: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِۦ نَفْسُهُۥ ۖ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ ٱلْوَرِيدِ
harf.verse: 50:16 — And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than [his] jugular vein.

deduction.title: The Burden of a Promise
deduction.intro: In the annals of faith, some names are mentioned as a testament to a single, profound quality. Today's figure is one such soul, whose very name is tied to a pledge of steadfastness. This individual is honored in the Quran for a character trait that serves as a lesson for all of humanity, earning a place among the most righteous. Can you uncover the identity of this paragon of patience and commitment?
clues: My story in the Quran is brief, yet my name is mentioned twice, listed among the patient and the righteous.
I am honored in one chapter alongside Ismail and Idris, remembered for our collective steadfastness.
My name itself is a title, signifying 'one of the portion' or 'one who took on a charge or guarantee'.
Allah explicitly states that He entered me into His mercy because I was truly among the righteous.
In another chapter, I am mentioned with Ismail and Al-Yasa' (Elisha) as one of the chosen and best.
I am Dhul-Kifl, and my entire Quranic legacy is captured in my unwavering patience and righteousness.

trial.label: What is my defining characteristic in the Quran?

location.label: I am mentioned alongside whom in Surah Al-Anbiya?

outcome.label: What was my reward mentioned in the Quran?
options: Being saved from a great fire
Being taught the speech of birds

deduction.arabic: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
deduction.verse: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient. (21:85)

translations: (the) misfortune (is because) of what have earned
scramble.hint: This verse connects accountability for one's actions with the vastness of Allah's mercy and forgiveness.
scramble.arabic: وَمَآ أَصَـٰبَكُم مِّن مُّصِيبَةٍۢ فَبِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَيَعْفُوا۟ عَن كَثِيرٍۢ
scramble.verseEn: And whatever strikes you of disaster - it is for what your hands have earned; but He pardons much.
